raw_consumer_statement:
Bank accepts word of creditors that something is a recurring payment, ie auto renewal at end of purchase term. They do not confirm with me if that is correct. 
After the subscription ends, they charge my card again. When I try to stop the payment the bank refuses. They say I need to contact the vendor. This has happened to me more than once! The vendor will not reverse the charge but might cancel for the next period but I am stuck paying for something I no longer wanted! 
This just happened again with an e-card company that I hardly used and forgot I even had. Then suddenly my card was charged {$50.00} to renew for another year! ( I had sent maybe XXXX or XXXX cards in the beginning but the recipients never got them! ) I called Citibank after trying to stop payment online. But in the past when I filed a despute the charge claim it was rejected as not a true dispute! I tried contacting the company but nothing ever happens. Some have no phone, only email which they ignored. I then asked the bank to give me a list of recurring charges attached to my account so I could possibly stop them before I was charged again. The bank refused to give me this information. So, even though I never authorized or tried to end the agreement, as the case may be, the bank will charge me again and once its charged will not stop payment on! So, I keep getting these surprise charges which I can not afford and have no way to avoid them until I have paid at least one renewal since I dont even know the renewal is in place! 
I would also like the bank to check with me to CONFIRM my agreement for these recurring charges not just accept the word of the business! If they can not reach me for confirmation, they should not be allowed to accept it.
